items.title: World-famous Horizontal Falls to allow more tourists, with cultural focus

children.content: Tourism operators, the state government and traditional owners strike an agreement about the future of tourism at the famed Horizontal Falls in WA's Kimberley.

gallery.altText: A huge set of bush clad sandstone peninsula with a gap in the middle showing ocean rushing through

thumbnail.altText: A huge set of bush clad sandstone peninsula with a gap in the middle showing ocean rushing through

items.title: Children and 75yo among dozens of homeless in major holiday town

children.content: A homeless survey conducted in one of WA's best-known holiday hotspots uncovers dozens of people sleeping rough, including a 75-year-old.

image.altText: the main street in Margaret River with parked cars lined on either side of the street

items.title: Massive hospital spend creates more questions than answers

children.content: The WA government is splashing an additional $1.5 billion on health, bringing the state's total hospital spend to $5.5 billion over the next four years. But where exactly will the extra money go?

image.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook wearing a white hard hat and yellow hi-ves vest speaks during a media conference at a construction site.

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: Shock loss of Emily has friends saying 'get your moles checked'

children.content: Emily avoided the sun and "did everything right". No-one considered that a melanoma removed from her shoulder in 2022 had spread to her brain.

gallery.altText: A group of people in black and purple clothing sit together on a bench. Shirts read "Remembering Em."
Two young women take a selfie together, both smiling at the camera, each with one hand under her chin.
Images of a young woman in glasses, smiling, painting a picture in a studio and holding a magazine at a table.

Images of a young woman in glasses, smiling, painting a picture in a studio and holding a magazine at a table.

items.title: Mystery of seventh man unsolved, months after Chinese boat arrivals

children.content: Months after the arrival of six Chinese nationals at Kalumburu in Western Australia's far north, questions remain about another man suspected of attempting to assist them.

gallery.altText: a graphic of people on a boat

thumbnail.altText: a graphic of people on a boat

items.title: As the Sussexes return to Australia, 'unusual' commercial engagements are raising eyebrows

children.content: Harry and Meghan will be travelling under their titles of Duke and Duchess of Sussex, and will appear at two major, ticketed events as well as making the rounds of various charities and causes.

image.altText: prince harry and his wife Meghan leaving a cathedral in the UK smiling and happy.

items.title: Social media checks for all visa applicants under Angus Taylor plan

children.content: Social media screening would be introduced for all visa applicants under an immigration policy to be revealed by Opposition Leader Angus Taylor.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie sits slumped in a chair, staring across a desk in front of him.

items.title: Tour operator cops $330K in cancellations as cyclone relief arrives for businesses

children.content: A whale shark boat skipper says one-off payments for tour operators will make a "small difference", as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle's aftermath drags on.

image.altText: A whale shark floats in clear water below a blue and white tour boat, with the name "Draw Card" on its bow.

items.title: Government announces billions in spending on military drones

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles says expanding Australia's fleet of autonomous and uncrewed systems will help keep the nation safe and boost Australia's sovereign defence industry.

items.title: HAZMAT warning lifted after blaze at Perth battery recycling facility

children.content: A man is taken to hospital after a serious fire at a lithium-ion battery recycling facility in Perth's south-eastern suburbs which also sparked a HAZMAT emergency warning.

items.title: Trump's 'AI Jesus' post sparks backlash as pope responds to attack

children.content: Donald Trump is criticised by religious sections of his supporter base after publishing, then deleting, the image, but the US president says: "I thought it was me as a doctor."

image.altText: A painting-style image showing Donald Trump in white robes resting his hand on the forehead of a hospital patient.

items.title: Terminally ill MP says ban on telehealth for assisted dying must change

children.content: A Labor politician living with MND has written to the attorney-general, calling for laws banning doctors from discussing voluntary assisted dying during telehealth appointments to be revised.

image.altText: Woman in read seated on set of stairs wearing a blue beanie.

items.title: Australia to join UK, France at summit on Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Australia will join more than 40 nations to discuss safeguarding shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, Labor's Pat Conroy has confirmed. Follow live.

image.altText: Pat Conroy at the dispatch box.

items.title: The neuroscience behind synaesthesia paints a surprising inner world

children.content: Have you ever tasted a word, or seen colours while listening to music? If you have, you may be among the 1 to 4 per cent of people who have a fascinating trait known as synaesthesia.

image.altText: Woman in headphones looks at bright spectrum of lights

items.title: Kitty Flanagan and Anne Edmonds create comedy chaos in new show

children.content: Plus Nicole Kidman is an Aussie wrestler in Margo's Got Money Troubles, Baby Reindeer's Richard Gadd returns, and the reboots keep on coming with Malcolm in the Middle.

gallery.altText: Two woman stand next to each other is a cluttered back room.

thumbnail.altText: Two woman stand next to each other is a cluttered back room.

items.title: Banks follow the money to cut off illicit tobacco after crackdown call

children.content: Australia's big banks have made hundreds of reports of suspicious activity related to illegal tobacco since the anti-money laundering regulator called for a crackdown late last year.

image.altText: Boxes of cigarettes surrounded by Australian cash.

items.title: Australia's LNG exporters will face a tougher future, new report says

children.content: The long-term outlook for Australia's liquefied natural gas exports is structurally constrained by declining demand, and the current war in the Middle East may hasten that shift away from gas, a new report claims.

image.altText: An LNG flare flames out from a stack.

items.title: Butters sent to Tribunal over 'abusive and insulting' umpire sledge

children.content: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters will front the AFL Tribunal over an exchange with umpire Nick Foot.

image.altText: Zak Butters reacts during Port's match against St Kilda

items.title: 'Missed opportunity': Astronomers lament loss of telescope partnership

children.content: The Artemis II mission used space technology developed in Australia, but experts fear the axing of a multimillion-dollar telescope partnership may stifle innovation and astronomy.

image.altText: Five buildings, four of which are firing lasers from their rooftops, against a starry Milky Way backdrop.

items.title: WA considering separate stockpile of diesel amid fuel shortages

children.content: The WA government is considering creating its own "strategic stockpile" of diesel, saying the state's size and the reliance of the agriculture and resources industries on the fuel warrants a separate reserve.

gallery.altText: A hand on a diesel pump at a petrol station.

thumbnail.altText: A hand on a diesel pump at a petrol station.

items.title: Calls for greater protections for Rottnest after Indigenous remains discovered

children.content: Aboriginal leaders call for greater protections for Rottnest Island after the discovery of Indigenous remains during construction work at the popular holiday resort last week.

image.altText: An aerial photo of the Bathurst Lighthouse in front of a series of premium tents at Rottnest Island.

items.title: Firefighter encouraging more women to join front line

children.content: Beelerup firefighter Kahlia Lloyd says joining the brigade was the most empowering thing she had done.

image.altText: Woman looking at camera standing in front of fire truck.

items.title: Caravan park residents evicted after change of ownership

children.content: Reg Hoyling thought his park home in Albany was where he would spend the rest of his life, but a sale of the caravan park meant he was among the tenants evicted.
